StringArrayType

  • iter

Dyn Compatibility

Implementors

In rerun::dataframe::external::re_chunk::external::arrow::compute::kernels::comparison

rerun::dataframe::external::re_chunk::external::arrow::compute::kernels::comparison

Trait StringArrayType

pub trait StringArrayType<'a>: Sized + ArrayAccessor<Item = &'a str> {
    // Required methods
    fn is_ascii(&self) -> bool;
    fn iter(&self) -> ArrayIter<Self> ⓘ;
}
Expand description

A trait for Arrow String Arrays, currently three types are supported:

  • StringArray
  • LargeStringArray
  • StringViewArray

This trait helps to abstract over the different types of string arrays so that we don’t need to duplicate the implementation for each type.

Required Methods§

fn is_ascii(&self) -> bool

Returns true if all data within this string array is ASCII

fn iter(&self) -> ArrayIter<Self> ⓘ

Constructs a new iterator

Dyn Compatibility§

This trait is not dyn compatible.

In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe.

Implementors§

§

impl<'a> StringArrayType<'a> for &'a GenericByteViewArray<StringViewType>

§

impl<'a, O> StringArrayType<'a> for &'a GenericByteArray<GenericStringType<O>>
where O: OffsetSizeTrait,
